questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education must an Iowa salesperson candidate complete?
questions.explanation: Iowa requires 60 hours of pre-license education for salesperson candidates. The coursework must be completed within 12 months of taking the licensing exam and must be from a Commission-approved school.

questions.question_text: A salesperson candidate completed her 60-hour pre-license course 14 months ago. Can she sit for the exam?
questions.correct_answer: No, the course expired after 12 months
distractors: Yes, she has 24 months to take test
Only if she completes a refresher
questions.explanation: Iowa requires the 60-hour salesperson pre-license course to be completed within 12 months of taking the exam. At 14 months, this candidate's education has expired and she must retake the course before sitting for the exam.

questions.question_text: A salesperson with 24 months of experience wants to become a broker. Within what timeframe must she complete broker education?
questions.correct_answer: Within 24 months of the broker exam
distractors: Within 12 months of the broker exam
questions.explanation: Iowa requires 60 hours of broker-specific education to be completed within 24 months of taking the broker exam. This longer window compared to salesperson pre-license acknowledges that broker candidates are working professionals.

questions.question_text: Which types of schools may offer Iowa pre-license real estate courses?
questions.explanation: All pre-license education in Iowa must come from schools approved by the Iowa Real Estate Commission. Both in-person and online formats are acceptable, but the provider must have Commission approval for the specific courses offered.

questions.question_text: Combining the 60-hour salesperson course of study and the broker program, how many exam-qualifying education hours must an Iowa broker complete?
questions.explanation: The salesperson course of study is 60 hours and the broker program is 60 additional hours, for 120 exam-qualifying hours. (Salesperson applicants also complete three 12-hour courses before applying for the original license, bringing total required coursework for a broker to 156 hours.)

questions.question_text: In addition to the 60-hour course, how many hours of coursework must an Iowa salesperson applicant complete before applying for a license?
questions.explanation: Iowa salesperson applicants must show evidence of three additional 12-hour live courses — Developing Professionalism and Ethical Practices, Buying Practices, and Listing Practices — when they apply for the original license. All required education must be completed during the 12 months before the application is submitted.

questions.question_text: A candidate passed the salesperson exam in March. When must her three 12-hour application courses be completed?
questions.correct_answer: Before she submits her license application
distractors: Within 12 months after she is licensed
questions.explanation: Evidence of the three 12-hour courses must accompany the original license application, and all required education must be completed during the 12 months before the application is submitted. The application itself is due by the last working day of the sixth month after passing the exam.

questions.question_text: Which three 12-hour courses must an Iowa salesperson applicant complete in addition to the 60-hour course?
questions.explanation: Iowa salesperson applicants complete three 12-hour live courses before applying for the original license: Developing Professionalism and Ethical Practices, Buying Practices, and Listing Practices. Together they add 36 hours to the 60-hour course of study.

questions.question_text: Including all required coursework before licensure, how many total hours must an Iowa salesperson applicant complete?
questions.explanation: An Iowa salesperson applicant completes the 60-hour course of study before the exam plus three 12-hour courses before the license application, totaling 96 hours. All of it must be completed during the 12 months before the application is submitted.

questions.question_text: An applicant passed the exam and completed Buying Practices and Listing Practices but skipped Professionalism/Ethics. Can she be licensed?
questions.correct_answer: No, all three courses are mandatory
distractors: Yes, two of three is sufficient
Yes, if she has 24 total hours
Only if she takes Ethics as CE later
questions.explanation: All three 12-hour courses are required with the original application: Developing Professionalism and Ethical Practices, Buying Practices, and Listing Practices. An application without evidence of all three cannot be approved.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum number of distance education hours allowed per Iowa CE renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Iowa allows a maximum of 24 distance education hours per 3-year renewal cycle. Since 36 total hours are required, at least 12 must be completed through live instruction (in-person or live webinar).

questions.question_text: A licensee earned 42 elective CE hours this cycle, needing only 24. How many can carry over?
questions.explanation: Iowa caps elective carryover at 18 hours per cycle. This licensee has 18 excess elective hours (42 minus 24), which equals exactly the maximum carryover. All 18 excess hours will transfer to the next cycle.

questions.question_text: A licensee completes 36 hours of CE entirely through online self-paced courses. Has she met Iowa's CE requirements?
questions.correct_answer: No, at least 12 hours must be live
distractors: No, all 36 must be live instruction
Only if her broker approves online
questions.explanation: Iowa allows a maximum of 24 distance education hours per 3-year renewal cycle. Since 36 total hours are required, at least 12 must be completed through live instruction such as in-person classes or live webinars.

questions.question_text: A licensee completed the 8-hour Law Update and has 28 hours of elective credit. What else must she complete this cycle?
distractors: The Law Update a second time
questions.explanation: Iowa requires three CE components: 8-hour Law Update (done), 4-hour Ethics (missing), and 24 hours elective (she has 28). Even though she has 36 total hours, she must still complete the mandatory 4-hour Ethics course specifically.

questions.question_text: Can mandatory CE courses like the Law Update carry over as elective credit to the next cycle?
questions.correct_answer: No, only elective hours carry over
distractors: Yes, all excess hours carry over
Yes, up to 8 hours can carry over
questions.explanation: Iowa's carryover provision applies only to unused elective CE hours, up to 18 hours maximum. Mandatory courses like the 8-hour Law Update and 4-hour Ethics must be completed fresh each renewal cycle and cannot carry over.
